title: What is QuickCrate Business and who is it for?
body: QuickCrate Business is a specialized account tier designed for offices, small businesses, restaurants, cafes, co-working spaces, and institutions that need to order supplies in bulk or on a recurring basis. It offers features not available to individual accounts: multi-user access under one billing entity, consolidated invoicing with GSTIN on every invoice, credit-based monthly billing (pay at month-end on invoice), higher COD and order-value limits, bulk quantity ordering on selected SKUs, dedicated account manager for large businesses, and a business-specific catalog with B2B pricing on high-volume items. QuickCrate Business is suitable for any organization spending ₹5,000 or more per month on supplies. There's no additional subscription fee for the basic Business account — sign up at quickcrate.in/business.

title: How do I sign up for a QuickCrate Business account?
body: Signing up for a QuickCrate Business account takes a few steps. Visit quickcrate.in/business and click 'Get Started'. Enter your business name, business type (company, LLP, proprietorship, etc.), registered address, and GSTIN. You'll need to upload proof of business registration (GST certificate, company incorporation certificate, or trade license). A QuickCrate Business onboarding agent will review your application and contact you within 2 business days. Upon approval, your account is upgraded to Business tier. If you already have a personal QuickCrate account, you can convert it to Business by going to Account → Business Account → Apply for Business. Your existing order history and wallet balance will be transferred. Business verification is required to prevent misuse of B2B pricing and credit billing.

title: How does monthly invoice billing work for QuickCrate Business?
body: QuickCrate Business accounts with an approved credit limit can place orders throughout the month and receive a consolidated tax invoice at the end of each billing cycle (typically the last day of the month). The invoice includes all orders placed during the month with a full itemized breakdown, GST split, and net payable. Payment of the invoice is due within 15 days of invoice date. Payment can be made via NEFT, RTGS, or online bank transfer to QuickCrate's business account. BNPL or UPI is also available for invoice settlement. Default on invoice payment results in credit suspension — orders revert to prepaid until the outstanding amount is cleared plus a reinstatement fee. Interest is charged at 1.5% per month on overdue amounts. Credit limits are set based on your business's monthly spend history and can be increased on request.

title: Can multiple employees place orders under one QuickCrate Business account?
body: Yes, QuickCrate Business supports multi-user access. The account owner (admin) can invite team members by going to Business Account → Team Management → Invite User. Enter the team member's email and phone number, assign them a role (Admin, Manager, or Employee), and set an optional monthly spend limit for their sub-account. Team members receive an invitation to link their QuickCrate personal account to the business account. Once linked, they can place orders that are charged to the business's consolidated billing or credit line. Admins can view all team orders in a unified dashboard, set per-user ordering permissions (which product categories they can buy), and export order data for expense reporting. All orders by team members generate GST-compliant invoices under the business's GSTIN.

title: Does QuickCrate Business offer bulk pricing?
body: Yes, QuickCrate Business accounts get access to B2B pricing on eligible high-volume SKUs. Bulk pricing is available for selected categories including office supplies, pantry staples, cleaning materials, and packaging goods. The B2B price is shown to logged-in Business account users on the product listing page (next to the regular price) and is automatically applied at checkout. Discounts range from 5-20% depending on the product and quantity ordered. Minimum quantity thresholds apply — typically ordering 6+ units of the same SKU triggers bulk pricing. Some products offer tiered discounts (e.g., 5% off for 6 units, 10% off for 12 units). Bulk pricing is negotiated with suppliers specifically for QuickCrate Business customers and is not available on personal accounts. Contact your dedicated account manager for custom pricing on very large orders (₹50,000+/month).

title: How do I get a GST invoice for my business orders?
body: All QuickCrate Business orders automatically generate GSTIN-linked GST-compliant tax invoices. Ensure your GSTIN is entered and verified in Business Account → Business Profile → GSTIN. Once verified, every invoice will include your GSTIN, enabling you to claim Input Tax Credit (ITC). Invoices are emailed to the registered business email within 30 minutes of delivery. You can also download invoices anytime from Business Account → Invoice History. For bulk invoice download (e.g., monthly or quarterly), use the 'Export All Invoices' feature which generates a ZIP file of all invoices in the selected date range. For ITC reconciliation, QuickCrate provides a GSTR-2B compatible data export (HSN code, taxable value, CGST, SGST, IGST columns) available under Business Account → GST Reports.

title: What delivery options are available for QuickCrate Business orders?
body: QuickCrate Business accounts enjoy all standard delivery options plus additional features. Standard express delivery (10-30 minutes) is available for all business orders within serviceable zones. For bulk orders exceeding 20 kg or containing large items, QuickCrate arranges a dedicated vehicle delivery — this requires advance scheduling (minimum 4 hours prior) via the Business Dashboard under 'Schedule Bulk Delivery'. Business accounts can set up recurring delivery schedules (e.g., every Monday morning for office supplies) under Business Account → Recurring Orders. Delivery to specific floors, departments, or individuals within a business premises can be specified in the delivery instructions. Business accounts in active zones also have access to a dedicated delivery executive (DE) who handles their regular orders during business hours, ensuring consistency and familiarity.

title: Can I integrate QuickCrate Business with our expense management tool?
body: Yes, QuickCrate Business supports integrations with popular expense management and accounting platforms. Available integrations include Zoho Books, Tally (via CSV export), SAP Business One (via API), and Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets via automated report export. To set up an integration, go to Business Account → Integrations → Connect Tool and follow the step-by-step guide for your platform. For Zoho Books, enter your Zoho credentials and QuickCrate automatically pushes invoices to your Zoho account after each delivery. Tally integration uses a custom XML ledger export available under Business Account → GST Reports → Export for Tally. For other ERP systems, QuickCrate provides a REST API with OAuth 2.0 authentication — contact your account manager for API documentation and a sandbox environment. Custom integrations are available for enterprise accounts.

title: What happens if my business order is delayed or has issues?
body: Business account orders are handled with priority support. If a business order is delayed or has quality issues (wrong items, damaged goods), your dedicated account manager is your first point of contact during business hours. You can also use the in-app Business Support channel, which connects you to a senior support agent with full visibility into your account. Business order issues are escalated and resolved with SLA targets: delivery issues acknowledged within 15 minutes, resolution within 2 hours for critical orders. For recurring supply disruptions (e.g., a staple product frequently going out of stock), your account manager can arrange pre-reservation of stock for your business. Refunds and replacements for business orders follow the same policy as personal accounts but are processed with higher priority.

title: How do I close or downgrade my QuickCrate Business account?
body: To close your QuickCrate Business account, ensure all outstanding invoices are paid and there are no pending orders or returns. Then contact your account manager or email business@quickcrate.in with a closure request. Business account closure is processed within 5 business days. Any credit balance in your QuickCrate Business Wallet is refunded to your registered bank account within 10 business days. After closure, your team members' linked personal accounts continue to function normally as individual accounts. To downgrade to a personal account (without full closure), go to Business Account → Manage Account → Downgrade to Personal. Downgrading removes multi-user access, B2B pricing, and credit billing immediately. GST invoice history remains accessible for 7 years post-closure for compliance purposes. Data export before closure is recommended.
